must_detect.matched_text:
incident summary — al barq holdings pjsc experienced a transient outage on the vendor onboarding portal hosted in adgm on 04 jun 2026 due to a misconfigured api key rotation.
scope — no customer accounts, emirates id copies, or family-origin fields were accessed; logs show only synthetic test profiles with invalid id strings such as eid 784-0000-0000000-0.
public context — similar configuration advisories have been circulated by sca and dfsa in 2023, and prior issues were announced on the adx disclosure feed by other issuers.
regulatory — the event falls below operational incident thresholds; per uae pdpl, no personal data breach notification is triggered based on current evidence.
detection and timeline — first alert at 02:14 gst; automated rollback completed 02:27 gst; vendor acknowledgement at 03:05 gst; service normalized by 03:20 gst.
impact — zero financial loss and no market-sensitive issuer information exposed; this was not a cyber-attack and not ransomware.
governance — board risk committee notified via role mailbox risk@albarq-holdings.example; no named persons included in the chain.
proof points — hash samples stored in a difc data room; public-source references appended (see sca circular 12/2021 and adgm guidance note 05/2022).
contract review — our term sheet templates include negated mac language for operational lapses that are promptly remediated and immaterial as to mae.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
